steps.title: Use point-slope form with the x-intercept
steps.content:
We know the line passes through $(-20, 0)$ with slope $m = -\frac{3}{4}$. Applying point-slope form with the point $(x_1, y_1) = (-20, 0)$:
$$y - 0 = -\frac{3}{4}(x - (-20))$$
$$y = -\frac{3}{4}(x + 20)$$

steps.title: Expand to slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Distribute the slope:
$$y = -\frac{3}{4}x - \frac{3}{4} \cdot 20$$
$$y = -\frac{3}{4}x - \frac{60}{4}$$
$$y = -\frac{3}{4}x - 15$$
The constant term is the y-intercept: $-15$.
